evals.prompt: Write unit tests for an AuthRepository class that has login(email, password) returning Future<User> and logout() returning Future<void>. It depends on an ApiClient. Cover success, failure, invalid input, and edge cases.
evals.expected_output: A test file using mocktail with MockApiClient, ProviderContainer if provider-based, 10+ test cases covering login success, login failure (401, network error), logout, empty email, empty password, null handling. Should use Unit mode.
expectations: The agent identifies this as Unit mode (repository/service class)
Uses mocktail (Mock, when, verify) — NOT mockito
Creates MockApiClient extending Mock
Has setUp with fresh mock and sut instances
Contains 10+ test cases covering happy path, error paths, edge cases
Uses AAA pattern with Arrange/Act/Assert comments
Test file path mirrors source: test/features/auth/data/auth_repository_test.dart

evals.prompt: Write tests for a CartNotifier Riverpod provider that manages a list of CartItem. It has addItem, removeItem, clearCart, and totalPrice getter. Test all state transitions.
evals.expected_output: Unit tests using ProviderContainer with addTearDown(container.dispose), testing all notifier methods and computed values. Should use Unit mode.
expectations: The agent identifies this as Unit mode (provider/notifier)
Uses ProviderContainer for provider testing
Calls addTearDown(container.dispose) for cleanup
Tests addItem, removeItem, clearCart methods
Tests totalPrice computation with various cart states
Tests edge cases: remove from empty cart, add duplicate item
Contains 10+ test cases

evals.prompt: Write tests for a UserService class with methods: fetchUsers() -> Future<List<User>>, getUserById(String id) -> Future<User?>, deleteUser(String id) -> Future<void>. It depends on a UserRepository.
evals.expected_output: Unit tests with MockUserRepository, covering all three methods with success, failure, empty results, and invalid IDs. Should use Unit mode.
expectations: The agent identifies this as Unit mode (service class)
Creates MockUserRepository with mocktail
Registers fallback values for custom types if needed
Tests fetchUsers returning empty list, single user, multiple users
Tests getUserById with valid ID, invalid ID, null result
Tests deleteUser success and failure scenarios
Uses group() to organize tests by method

evals.prompt: Write widget tests for a LoginScreen that has email field, password field, login button, and shows error messages on validation failure. It uses a Riverpod AuthNotifier for state.
evals.expected_output: Widget tests using testWidgets, pumpWidget with ProviderScope overrides, finder patterns, interaction testing. Should use Widget mode.
expectations: The agent identifies this as Widget mode (screen/UI)
Uses testWidgets and tester.pumpWidget with ProviderScope
Overrides authNotifierProvider with mock or test state
Tests rendering of email field, password field, login button
Tests validation error display on empty/invalid input
Tests tap interactions triggering state changes
Uses find.byKey, find.text, or find.byType finders

evals.prompt: Write widget tests for a ProductCard component that displays product name, price, and an 'Add to Cart' button. Verify rendering, tap behavior, and different product states (in stock, out of stock, on sale).
evals.expected_output: Widget tests for a reusable component with multiple render states and interaction verification. Should use Widget mode.
expectations: The agent identifies this as Widget mode (component)
Tests rendering of product name, price, and button
Tests 'Add to Cart' button tap calls the expected callback
Tests out-of-stock state disables the button or shows alternate text
Tests on-sale state shows discounted price or sale badge
Uses const Key for widget lookups
Has 10+ test cases covering all visual states

evals.prompt: Write golden tests for a ProfileCard widget to verify its visual appearance. The card shows avatar, name, email, and a settings icon. Test both light and dark themes.
evals.expected_output: Widget tests with matchesGoldenFile assertions for visual regression testing. Should use Widget mode with golden file patterns.
expectations: The agent identifies this as Widget mode (golden/visual test)
Uses matchesGoldenFile for visual assertions
Tests both light and dark theme variants
Wraps widget in MaterialApp with appropriate ThemeData
Provides instructions for running with --update-goldens flag
Golden file paths follow convention: goldens/profile_card_light.png
Sets fixed surface size for deterministic screenshots

evals.prompt: Write integration tests for a complete login-to-home flow: user opens app, sees login screen, enters credentials, taps login, gets navigated to home screen, sees welcome message with their name.
evals.expected_output: Integration tests using IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding with full app launch, form interaction, navigation verification. Should use Integration mode.
expectations: The agent identifies this as Integration mode (full app flow)
Uses IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized()
Launches full app with tester.pumpWidget
Tests sequential flow: login screen → enter credentials → tap login → home screen
Verifies navigation occurred (finds HomeScreen or home content)
Uses pumpAndSettle after navigation transitions
Test file placed in integration_test/ directory

evals.prompt: Write integration tests for bottom navigation: verify tapping each tab (Home, Search, Profile) switches to the correct screen and maintains state when switching back.
evals.expected_output: Integration tests verifying tab navigation and state preservation across screen switches. Should use Integration mode.
expectations: The agent identifies this as Integration mode (navigation test)
Tests tapping each bottom nav icon navigates to correct screen
Verifies state is preserved when switching tabs and returning
Uses find.byIcon or find.byKey for tab buttons
Calls pumpAndSettle after each navigation
Asserts correct screen type or content is visible after each tap
Tests at least 3 tab switches with back-and-forth navigation
